# Yvonne's Wardrobe — Product Requirements Document

*Reflects app version v10.9 · June 2026*

## 1. Overview
Yvonne's Wardrobe is a mobile-first digital closet app. It lets the user photograph and catalogue every clothing item they own, mix and match outfits by swiping tops and bottoms independently (or browsing one-pieces), and plan outfits ahead on a calendar by day type. The visual style is a modern take on the *Clueless* "Cher's Closet" Y2K aesthetic.

## 2. Goals
- Digitise the wardrobe with a photo of every piece.
- Make daily outfit decisions fast and fun via swipe-to-combine.
- Plan outfits ahead by date and day type, with smart recommendations.
- Deliver a distinctive, joyful Y2K/Clueless look rather than a generic utility app.

## 3. Target user
- **Primary:** the owner, managing her own wardrobe on her phone.
- **Secondary (future):** friends each running their own copy/closet, if shared (gated on auth — see §10).

## 4. Platform & technical approach
- Single self-contained **HTML file** — HTML, CSS, and JavaScript all inline, no build step, no framework.
- Runs in any mobile or desktop browser; **installable to the home screen** (PWA manifest + apple-touch-icon, which also avoids Safari's storage eviction).
- Web fonts from Google Fonts (Silkscreen, Rubik Mono One, Sniglet).
- **Data storage (local-first):** the source of truth is **IndexedDB** on the device — item metadata plus photo blobs. An in-memory/demo fallback covers environments where IndexedDB is unavailable.
- **Optional cloud backup:** Supabase (Postgres + Storage bucket `wardrobe-photos`), reached through manual **Back Up / Restore** actions. It is strictly opt-in — the shared file ships with **no embedded credentials**, so anyone given the app gets a fully local closet with no visibility into the owner's data.
- **Credentials** (Supabase URL + anon key) are entered per-device in a settings modal and saved only in that device's `localStorage`, never in the app file.
- **Personalisation (v10.7):** on first run the app asks for the user's name and stores it per-device in `localStorage` (key `yw-owner`). The name drives the header logo ("Jola's Wardrobe"), the browser tab title, and the home-screen install name. It is purely a display value — **not** a database field — so it has no schema impact and stays on-device like the cloud credentials. Tapping the logo re-opens the prompt to rename.
- Uploaded photos are downscaled (~680px) and compressed (WebP) for storage.

## 5. Two-layer category model
The app separates **clothing tags** (the vibe of a garment) from **calendar day types** (the occasions you plan around). Clothing tags map up into day types, which drives the day styler's recommendations.

**Clothing tags (My Stuff):** Casual · Work · Going out · Formal · Active · Holiday
**Calendar day types (plan ahead):** Work · Social · Travel · Active

| Day type | Day styler recommends clothing tagged |
|---|---|
| Work | Work |
| Social | Going out, Formal |
| Travel | Casual, Active, Holiday |
| Active | Active |

This mapping was **tightened in v10.4**. Earlier versions let "Casual" map into Work and Social as well, which meant nearly every item passed the filter and the day styler wasn't actually narrowing anything — a filter that doesn't narrow is worse than no filter. Work now recommends only Work, and Social only Going out / Formal.

Casual is deliberately **not** a day type (an unplanned day is casual by default); "Night out" and "Formal" are folded into **Social**. A "show all my clothes" override lets the user pick outside the recommended set for any day.

## 6. Features (current — v10.9)

### 6.1 Closet (swipe)
- Two modes via toggle: **Separates** (Tops + Bottoms on independent swipe decks) and **One Pieces**.
- **Top item name above the garment (v10.9):** in the Separates view, the top's name now sits above its stage instead of below, so the eye reads name → garment → garment in one downward sweep and the full top-and-bottom outfit is visible together without the name interrupting the silhouette. A small gap (margin) was added between the name and the garment, and the empty space below shorter tops was trimmed.
- **One-piece view (v10.8):** the redundant "ONE PIECES" deck label was removed (the mode toggle already says it), and the dress stage was enlarged with a gap above the garment so a one-piece hangs from near the top and fills more of the screen — matching the label-free, fuller treatment of the separates decks.
- **Prev/next arrows sit on the left/right edges of each garment** (not in a row between them), so the top and bottom stack reads as one whole outfit; swipe still works too.
- **Stage proportions (v10.5):** the closet is split roughly **35% top / 65% bottom**, sized to nearly fill the screen, so the top-and-bottom stack reads as a real silhouette rather than two equal tiles.
- **Garment length / proportional rendering (v10.6):** each garment fills a fraction of its stage based on its optional **length** tag (see §6.2 and §8), so a cropped top doesn't balloon to the same height as a long coat. Untagged items fall back to a sensible default fill, so nothing looks broken before the user has tagged lengths.
- Top/Bottom text labels removed from the closet — position makes it obvious.
- **Shuffle 🎲:** randomises the current combination (formerly "Style Me").
- **Plan It:** sends the current look to the calendar to drop on a chosen day.

### 6.2 My Stuff
- **Add a piece is always reachable (v10.9):** the CTA now lives in its own band directly under the header, outside the scrolling closet grid — so it stays visible and tappable no matter how far the user has scrolled, on any device. (Earlier `position: sticky` approaches were unreliable in some mobile browsers; this version sidesteps that by structurally keeping the control out of the scrolling area rather than trying to pin it within it.) Tapping the centred **"＋ ADD A PIECE"** header still expands the form inline (photo from camera/gallery, name, category, **optional length**, season + occasion tags), and it auto-collapses after saving.
- **Season, simplified (v10.9):** season tags are now **Summer or Winter only** (Spring and Fall were removed as options). Tagging remains optional — items can be left season-less and will show under any season filter. This keeps the tag meaningful (warm vs. cold) rather than a fuzzy four-way guess.
- **Length chips (v10.6):** an optional single-select length picker whose options depend on the chosen category (top: cropped/regular/long; bottom: mini/knee/midi/ankle/full; dress: mini/knee/midi/maxi). It prompts "pick a type first" until a category is selected, and clears itself if the category changes to one where the value no longer applies.
- Grid of all pieces; **tap a piece to edit** its name, length, and tags. **Delete moved into the edit sheet (v10.8):** the thumbnail no longer carries a × button (it was too easy to delete by accident); deleting now happens via a **🗑 Delete this piece** button inside an opened item, behind a confirm prompt.
- **Filters:** by type, season, and occasion, combinable (any-of within a group, and-across groups). Season filter now offers Summer/Winter only, matching the tagging options.
- **Backup & sync** lives behind a **☁️ icon in the top-right of the header** (reachable from any screen, any scroll position). It opens a modal with Back Up / Restore and the optional Database API fields. Copy: *"Database API is optional. If left blank, your data is stored on this device."* The icon shows a connected state once a backup target is set.

### 6.3 Calendar
- Month view, **weeks starting Monday**; assign an outfit to any day.
- **Day types:** Work, Social, Travel, Active — colour-coded with a legend.
- Tap a day to set its type, view/clear the planned outfit. The day's planned look is shown as a **vertically stacked top-over-bottom preview** (v10.5), using the same length-based proportions as the closet.
- **Style a day:** from a day's planner, open a styler showing pieces filtered to that day type's recommended tags (swipe tops/bottoms/one-pieces, shuffle, or toggle "show all my clothes"), then save the look to that day. Deck labels were removed from the Style Me modal in v10.5 to match the closet.

### 6.4 Personalisation — owner name (v10.7)
- On first open, a styled overlay asks "Whose closet is this?" and the entered name personalises the header logo (e.g. **JOLA'S WARDROBE**), the page title, and the PWA install name.
- The name is required on first run, stored per-device in `localStorage`, and the prompt does not reappear on later visits.
- **Tap the logo** at any time to re-open the prompt and rename (covers typos and shared/handed-on devices).
- Display-only and device-local: it is never written to the database and has no schema impact. This makes the app friend-ready — anyone given the file gets their own named, fully local closet with no access to the owner's data.

## 7. Seed wardrobe (ships with the app)
On a first run with an empty closet, the app loads these **5 starter pieces** so the swipe, shuffle, and planning flows work immediately. They persist on-device and can be edited or deleted like any item. (They do not overwrite an existing wardrobe — a device that already has data keeps it.)

<table>
<tr>
<td align="center"><img src="data:image/webp;base64," width="90" alt="rust tie-front top"></td>
<td align="center"><img src="data:image/webp;base64," width="90" alt="striped tie-waist blouse"></td>
<td align="center"><img src="data:image/webp;base64," width="90" alt="taupe wide-leg trousers"></td>
<td align="center"><img src="data:image/webp;base64," width="90" alt="black maxi skirt"></td>
<td align="center"><img src="data:image/webp;base64," width="90" alt="black puff-sleeve dress"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td align="center">rust tie-front top</td>
<td align="center">striped tie-waist blouse</td>
<td align="center">taupe wide-leg trousers</td>
<td align="center">black maxi skirt</td>
<td align="center">black puff-sleeve dress</td>
</tr>
</table>

| Piece | Type | Length | Season | Occasion |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| rust tie-front top | top | long | — (none) | Going out, Casual |
| striped tie-waist blouse | top | regular | Winter | Casual, Work, Going out |
| taupe wide-leg trousers | bottom | full | Winter | Work, Going out, Formal |
| black maxi skirt | bottom | full | Summer | Casual, Going out, Formal |
| black puff-sleeve dress | one piece | knee | Summer | Going out, Formal |

*(Updated in v10.9: seed items previously carried Spring/Fall season tags left over from before the season system was restricted to Summer/Winter. Each item's season was reassigned to the closest fit, or left blank, so the starter wardrobe stays consistent with the new tagging options. Note the seed table also corrects a v10.7 drift — that release changed the rust top's length to `long`, but this table still showed `cropped`.)*

## 8. Data model
- **item:** id, name, category (`top` | `bottom` | `dress`), **length (optional)**, season[], occasion[], image, created date. (Metadata is stored in IndexedDB; the photo blob is keyed by item id.)
- **length (optional, added v10.6):** a single per-item tag whose vocabulary depends on category — top: `cropped` | `regular` | `long`; bottom: `mini` | `knee` | `midi` | `ankle` | `full`; dress: `mini` | `knee` | `midi` | `maxi`. It maps to a fill fraction of the display stage (e.g. cropped top ≈ 0.68, full bottom = 1.0) so garments render at believable relative heights. Stored as `null` when untagged; the renderer then uses a per-category default (top→regular, bottom→ankle, dress→midi) so untagged items still look right.
- **day plan (per date):** day_type plus the planned outfit — `{ topId, botId }` for separates or `{ dressId }` for a one-piece.
- *Supabase shape (for backup/sync):* `items` table (now including a nullable `length` column) + `day_plans` table; photo files in Storage bucket `wardrobe-photos`. A legacy `outfits` table from an early draft is superseded by `day_plans` and flagged for cleanup. See accompanying `Yvonnes-Wardrobe-schema.sql`.

## 9. Non-functional requirements
- Mobile-first, comfortable one-hand use.
- Accessibility: improved colour contrast and larger text while preserving the Y2K style (acknowledged trade-off: the pixel display font is less legible than a plain font by design).
- Offline: works offline except first-load web fonts; local data persists on-device via IndexedDB.
- Privacy by default: no credentials in the shared file; sharing the app never shares the owner's data.

## 10. Current limitations
- Cloud sync is **manual backup/restore only** — there is no automatic live sync yet.
- Supabase is run with **RLS off intentionally** until authentication exists; it only provides meaningful protection once there are distinct logged-in users. Treated as a hard gate before any sharing.
- One day type per day; one top + one bottom per separates look (no layering, shoes, or accessories yet).
- No login / multi-user; with Supabase added but no auth, access is open via the anon key.
- Recommendations are only as good as the user's tagging.

## 11. Roadmap
- **Supabase live sync** (automatic cloud data + photo storage), building on the existing manual backup.
- Backup **export/import** (CSV / JSON).
- **Authentication + row-level security** — required before sharing publicly.
- Public hosting for sharing (GitHub Pages, `index.html`).
- Multi-tag days; shoes/accessories/layering; outfit suggestions by day type or weather.
- Strategic decision (open): remain a personal curated product vs. become a shared product with external users.

## 12. Version history
- **v2** — base: Clueless/Y2K UI, swipe decks, My Stuff, calendar, real preview photos.
- **v4** — wording (Style Me, One Piece), gallery upload, tap-to-edit items.
- **v5** — UX contrast pass, My Closet filters, calendar day types + legend.
- **v6 / v7** — two-layer model: clothing tags ↔ day-type mapping, day styler with recommended filter + "show all" override.
- **v8–v9.1** — local-first re-architecture: **IndexedDB** as source of truth; optional Supabase backup via manual Back Up / Restore; per-device credentials in a settings panel; PWA install metadata; seed garments baked in.
- **v10** — closet arrows moved to garment sides; bottoms deck taller; Top/Bottom labels removed; "Style Me" → **"Shuffle 🎲"**; **calendar starts Monday**.
- **v10.1** — bottoms +10% more; **Add a piece** collapsed behind a CTA; backup/restore/settings tucked away; simplified copy.
- **v10.2** — Add a piece reworked as one unified expanding section; **settings moved to a header ☁️ icon + modal** (always reachable, full-contrast).
- **v10.3** — **5-piece seed wardrobe** (rust top, striped blouse, taupe trousers, black maxi skirt, black puff-sleeve dress).
- **v10.4** — real garment **cutout images** processed and embedded as data URIs in the seed function (loads on a fresh install without overwriting existing data); **day-styler OCC_MAP tightened** so the recommended set actually narrows (Work→Work, Social→Going out/Formal).
- **v10.5** — **layout pass:** closet and Style Me stages resized to ~35% / 65% top-to-bottom; deck labels removed from the Style Me modal; calendar day-outfit preview switched to a **vertical stacked** layout.
- **v10.6** — optional per-item **length** tag (top: cropped/regular/long · bottom: mini/knee/midi/ankle/full · dress: mini/knee/midi/maxi) mapped to stage fill fractions, fixing garment proportions; untagged items fall back to a per-category default fill.
- **v10.7** — **first-run name prompt** personalises the header logo, page title, and PWA install name (stored per-device in `localStorage`, tap-logo to rename) — making the app friend-ready as a self-contained local copy. Seed wardrobe tweak: rust tie-front top length changed `cropped` → `long`. No schema change (the name is display-only and device-local).
- **v10.8** — **one-piece view** cleaned up: removed the "ONE PIECES" deck label (closet + Style Me modal) and enlarged the dress stage with a top gap so one-pieces fill more of the screen; **delete moved off the My Stuff thumbnail into the edit sheet** (behind a confirm) to stop accidental deletes. No schema change.
- **v10.9** — **Closet:** top item name moved above the garment (was below) for a clearer full-set view, with new spacing tuned between name/garment and less empty space below short tops. **Season:** restricted tagging and filtering to **Summer/Winter only** (Spring/Fall removed); tagging stays optional. Seed wardrobe's leftover Spring/Fall tags reassigned to match. **My Stuff:** the **Add a piece** CTA was restructured to live outside the scrolling area so it's always reachable regardless of scroll position (previous `position: sticky` approach proved unreliable in testing); CTA header copy is now centre-aligned. No schema change — all changes are UI/markup only.

## 13. Project status & handoff
- **Current build:** `yvonnes-wardrobe-v10.9.html` (the app); previous versions retained for rollback.
- **Storage:** local-first on IndexedDB; Supabase optional. Supabase project: Data API on, **RLS off** (intentional until auth), region Asia-Pacific, bucket `wardrobe-photos` + tables from the schema SQL. Note: projects created after Supabase's May 2026 defaults change need an explicit `grant usage on schema public to anon, authenticated` + `notify pgrst, 'reload schema'` for PostgREST to pick up changes; Storage enforces its own RLS, so a public bucket grants read only — uploads need explicit storage policies.
- **Schema note (v10.6):** the `items` table now carries a nullable `length` column. The accompanying `Yvonnes-Wardrobe-schema.sql` includes it in the table definition plus a one-line `alter table ... add column if not exists length text;` migration for any existing project — run it (and reload the PostgREST schema) before wiring live sync, since a write of `length` to a column that doesn't exist will fail.
- **Immediate next steps:**
  1. Wire **live** Supabase sync (item reads/writes + photo uploads), building on manual backup; ensure the `length` column is migrated first.
  2. Add Export/Import backup.
  3. Authentication + RLS before any sharing or public hosting.
- **Files to carry into a new chat:** `yvonnes-wardrobe-v10.9.html`, this PRD, and `Yvonnes-Wardrobe-schema.sql`.
